title: President  Gotabaya and his Former  Prime  Minister Mahinda rarely confronted each other directly, yet they disagreed on everything, including high-stakes political gamesmanship and petty corruption
body_lines: The mob was bashing on the gates of the Sri Lankan prime minister’s official residence, its size and fury swelling dangerously.

For weeks, Mahinda Rajapaksa, the 76-year-old prime minister, had been under pressure to resign as the economy imploded and protests erupted. The brother of the president, Gotabaya, and a patriarch of his own political dynasty, Mahinda was once hailed as appachchi, the beloved father of the people. Now he was huddled in his second-floor bedroom, accompanied by relatives who frantically called army officers, pleading to be rescued.

Outside the gates, anti-government protesters who had been attacked earlier by Mahinda’s supporters were taking their revenge — rioting, burning buses and torching hundreds of homes owned by allies of the Rajapaksas. A lawmaker from their party was beaten to death, his body dragged through the streets.
That day, May 9, was one of the most violent and chaotic in recent Sri Lankan history. But it was precipitated by years of turmoil inside the house of Rajapaksa.

The Rajapaksa brothers have dominated politics here for most of the last 20 years. After helping Mahinda win the presidency in 2005, his brothers Chamal, Gotabaya and Basil took over ministries that controlled three-quarters of the national budget and built popular support despite allegations of human rights abuses and corruption. But by 2019, when Gotabaya became president, the family was marred by infighting and dysfunction that would drive South Asia’s most developed nation into ruin.

In interviews, current and former ministers, foreign diplomats and Rajapaksa confidants, some of whom spoke for the first time as they saw the family splinter, said Gotabaya and Mahinda, and their respective factions, clashed over ministerial appointments and agricultural policies, investment deals and political favors. As the economy went into free fall this year, Mahinda, backed by several Rajapaksa scions, resisted Gotabaya’s wish that he step aside.

Distrust deepened to the point that members of Mahinda’s inner circle, besieged in his compound May 9, felt that the president had abandoned them. Udayanga Weeratunga, a cousin who was with the prime minister, and another family aide who was present, told The Washington Post that they suspect Gotabaya’s supporters in the army purposefully delayed coming to their aid for six hours.

Gotabaya is clinging to power after replacing his brother with a new prime minister, who revealed this week that Sri Lanka has less than $1 million in foreign reserves, dwindling medical supplies and almost no fuel.
Sri Lanka faces “total destruction,” former president Maithripala Sirisena said. “The country has learned a lesson about dynastic politics.”

When Mahinda, the son of a wealthy rice and coconut farmer who was active in politics, ran for parliament in 1970, he was following in the tradition of the few elite families that dominate Sri Lanka, a lush teardrop-shape island off the coast of India.

“You cannot win [in politics] if you’re not from an established family,” said Razeen Sally, a professor at the National University of Singapore. “So the system is left to established insiders who can pillage the state.”

The second of nine children, Mahinda was charismatic, loved crowds and stuck close to his younger brother, Basil, who is considered the family’s political strategist. Their middle brother, Gotabaya, was always different: aloof, politically inexperienced, a teetotaler and vegetarian who spent 21 years in the military. “He would visit the ancestral home only during New Year,” recalled Weeratunga, their cousin who is close to Mahinda.

The Rajapaksas ran the country like a family business during Mahinda’s 10-year presidency, starting in 2005. He named Gotabaya defense secretary while Basil and their oldest brother, Chamal, were placed in charge of irrigation and economic development. Sri Lanka enjoyed years of growth, fueled by a mountain of foreign debt.
Mahinda enjoyed the adulation of voters, who approved of his bloody but decisive victory in a 26-year civil war against Tamil rebels and his frequent appeals to Sinhalese Buddhist nationalism.

But allegations of corruption, including questionable deals with Chinese state companies and officials, swirled around Mahinda. Gotabaya was also implicated, though to a lesser extent, and faced scrutiny over the 2006 purchase of MiG fighters from Ukraine.

Sankhitha Gunaratne, deputy executive director of Transparency International Sri Lanka, said Mahinda and Basil have faced numerous accusations, including diverting tsunami relief aid and using public funds to buy land, but many cases have stalled or been withdrawn. “The alleged Rajapaksa corruption is like a large tree that provides shade to many people,” she said.
In 2021, a leaked trove of financial documents known as the Pandora Papers revealed that a niece of the Rajapaksa brothers had millions of dollars hidden in offshore accounts.

Amid growing anger over the Rajapaksas’ alleged cronyism and corruption, Mahinda lost a bid for a third term in 2015. Almost immediately, an eclectic coalition of pro-Western business executives, military hard-liners and Buddhist monks identified a new candidate: Gotabaya Rajapaksa.

It quickly became apparent that Gotabaya, backed by new political sponsors, would clash with Mahinda. The men rarely confronted each other directly, yet they disagreed on everything, including high-stakes political gamesmanship and petty corruption, family confidants said.

Dilith Jayaweera, a media magnate who is widely credited with launching Gotabaya’s candidacy, remembers an incident from 2018 when he was called by Mahinda to Gotabaya’s home. Mahinda had put Gotabaya’s name on the title to an illegally built resort so that a powerful monk, a political ally, could get free electricity. The scandal was about to leak and, as was often the case, Mahinda was reluctant to tell his brother, so he nudged Jayaweera to break the news to him.
Gotabaya was “livid,” Jayaweera said, and stormed off to a Buddhist temple, refusing to share a car with his brother.

In October 2018, a constitutional crisis erupted when Sirisena, then president, fired his prime minister, Ranil Wickremesinghe, and replaced him with Mahinda, who he had defeated at the polls just three years earlier. The capital was tense as both men made claims on the country’s No. 2 job and rumors swirled that Wickremesinghe might be removed by force.
Fearing Mahinda and Basil were trying to outflank him and engineer their own return to power, Gotabaya secretly met Wickremesinghe to pledge his support.
Soon after, the Supreme Court ruled against Mahinda’s claim, and he backed down. The family had no option but to support Gotabaya.

In the run-up to elections, terrorist attacks by Muslim extremists rocked Sri Lanka, galvanizing Sinhalese Buddhist support around the former military man. On the campaign trail, Gotabaya spoke of security, good governance and development, pitching himself as a technocrat, and Colombo, with its emerging skyline of Indian- and Chinese-funded skyscrapers, as the next Singapore. He won in a landslide.

On the day of his swearing-in on Nov. 19, 2019, Gotabaya signaled a break from his family. He refused to wear a red “sataka,” the Rajapaksa clan’s signature scarf, favoring a short-sleeved shirt. Unlike Mahinda, who printed his own image on 1,000-rupee notes while he was president, Gotabaya prohibited government offices from hanging his official portrait.
But the next day was “the beginning of the downfall,” said Nalaka Godahewa, a former financial executive who was later Gotabaya’s minister of mass media.

Gotabaya’s pro-Western business-sector backers had recommended a list of appointments, but when the president unveiled his first Cabinet, it was led by Mahinda as prime minister and stocked with Basil and Mahinda loyalists. They enacted steep tax cuts and argued against seeking aid from the International Monetary Fund despite mounting debt. Gotabaya personally pushed through a ban on chemical fertilizers that hurt crop yields, just as global food prices soared.

Mahinda’s supporters said they had shaped the Cabinet only to be undermined by Gotabaya’s appointments. In several instances, the government issued trade policies that were retracted within 24 hours. “You had ministers fighting secretaries,” Weeratunga said. “Fighting permeated the administration.”
The Rajapaksas were united on one issue: A constitutional amendment passed in 2020 that weakened commissions investigating corruption and granted the president far-reaching powers over the courts.
By early 2022, the economy was in free fall. Foodstuffs like rice doubled in price from a year prior. Gasoline and electricity were in short supply. Foreign currency reserves were running out.

In April, nightly demonstrations took root in the capital demanding that the Rajapaksas leave politics, and some turned violent. Gotabaya’s entire Cabinet — which included Basil, the finance minister; the elder brother, Chamal; and Mahinda’s son — resigned, giving Gotabaya a chance to form a new government. Sri Lanka needed a stable image to present to foreign lenders and negotiate an urgent bailout.
But Mahinda, the prime minister, resisted calls from the opposition and even signals from the president to quit.

Gotabaya didn’t force the issue. “G.R. would say, ‘He knows what I want,’ ” said Godahewa, who joined the Cabinet after several Rajapaksas departed. “He felt he needed the support of Basil and Mahinda.”

With pressure mounting on Mahinda, his supporters organized a May 9 rally at Temple Trees, the prime minister’s compound. The patriarch, feeling deflated and mulling resigning, suddenly seemed energized, according to two family insiders and videos of the event.

“As a leader who has always listened to the people, I now ask you: What needs to be done?” Mahinda said to thousands of supporters sitting cross-legged in a chandelier-lit hall. “You must stay!” the crowd roared. “Does that mean I shouldn’t resign?” he asked again, soaking in calls to fight on.
When the rally ended, supporters streamed out of Temple Trees with steel rods and wooden sticks, beating anti-government protesters and sparking a bloody backlash that shocked the nation.

Holed up in Temple Trees with his sons, who had urged him to stay, Mahinda told his speechwriter at 4 p.m. that he was resigning. The speechwriter spread the news to the media, but that didn’t stop the violence, said two people inside the compound.
Despite the family’s pleas, the army didn’t send reinforcements until 11 p.m., after protesters had already breached a gate. At 4 a.m., Mahinda was evacuated by soldiers to a military base.

“Mahinda understood this stalling was deliberate,” said Weeratunga, who accused Gotabaya of trying to intimidate his brothers. But two ministers who were by the president’s side that day said he furiously called military officers to no avail.
“He could neither control the army nor police,” Godahewa said.

Godahewa and foreign diplomats said army commander Shavendra Silva — who has been in frequent touch with Western officials — was reluctant to deploy his forces for fear of being seen as ordering a military crackdown.

The absence of the military that day widened the fissures among the brothers. In a speech to parliament this week, Chamal chastised Mahinda for not leaving politics in 2015. And at a recent meeting of the Rajapaksas’ party members, family allies angrily asked why they were not protected on May 9 in a rare display of discord. “How Gotabaya treats the party now will decide the direction of the people’s wrath,” Weeratunga said.
On May 12, an embattled and isolated Gotabaya named a new prime minister: Ranil Wickremesinghe, the man he secretly met with in 2018 when he first jockeyed for the position against his brother.

Four years later, Sri Lanka’s most powerful family was crumbling — maybe for good, said Jayaweera, the media magnate.“The Rajapaksas, and Sri Lanka, ended in tragedy,” he said. “It ended because of their own doing.”

si.title: ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය සහ ඔහුගේ හිටපු අගමැති මහින්ද එකිනෙකාට සෘජුව මුහුණ දුන්නේ කලාතුරකිනි, නමුත් ඉහළ දේශපාලන ක්‍රීඩාව සහ සුළු දූෂණ ඇතුළු සෑම දෙයකටම ඔවුන් එකඟ නොවීය.
body_lines: කල්ලිය ශ්‍රී ලංකා අගමැති නිල නිවසේ දොරටුවලට පහර දෙමින් සිටි අතර, එහි විශාලත්වය සහ කෝපය භයානක ලෙස ඉදිමීම.

76 හැවිරිදි අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂට සති ගනනාවක් තිස්සේ ආර්ථිකය කඩාවැටීම සහ විරෝධතා පැන නැගීම හේතුවෙන් ඉල්ලා අස්වන ලෙසට බලපෑම් එල්ල විය. ජනාධිපතිගේ සොහොයුරා වන ගෝඨාභය සහ ඔහුගේම දේශපාලන පරම්පරාවේ කුලදෙටුවෙකු වූ මහින්ද කලෙක ජනතාවගේ ආදරණීය පියා අප්පච්චි ලෙසින් හඳුන්වයි. දැන් ඔහු තම දෙවන මහලේ නිදන කාමරයේ ගුලි වී සිටි අතර, ඥාතීන් සමඟ වියරුවෙන් හමුදා නිලධාරීන් ඇමතූ අතර, ඔවුන් බේරා දෙන ලෙස ඉල්ලා සිටියේය.

ගේට්ටුවෙන් පිටතදී, මහින්දගේ ආධාරකරුවන්ගේ ප්‍රහාරයට ලක් වූ ආණ්ඩු විරෝධී විරෝධතාකරුවන් ඔවුන්ගේ පළිගැනීම් කරමින් සිටියහ - කැරලි කෝලාහල, බස් ගිනි තැබීම සහ රාජපක්ෂලාගේ හිතවතුන්ට අයත් නිවාස සිය ගණනක් ගිනිබත් කරමින්. ඔවුන්ගේ පක්ෂයේ නීති සම්පාදකයෙකුට පහර දී මරා දමා, ඔහුගේ දේහය වීදි දිගේ ඇදගෙන ගියේය.
එම දිනය, එනම් මැයි 9, මෑත කාලීන ශ්‍රී ලංකා ඉතිහාසයේ වඩාත්ම ප්‍රචණ්ඩකාරී සහ අවුල්සහගත දිනයකි. නමුත් රාජපක්ෂගේ නිවස තුළ වසර ගණනාවක් පැවති කැළඹිලි හේතුවෙන් එය වේගවත් විය.

පසුගිය වසර 20 න් වැඩි කාලයක් මෙහි දේශපාලනය පාලනය කළේ රාජපක්ෂ සහෝදරයන්ය. 2005 දී මහින්දට ජනාධිපති කරවීමට උදව් කිරීමෙන් පසු ඔහුගේ සොහොයුරන් වන චමල්, ගෝඨාභය සහ බැසිල් ජාතික අයවැයෙන් හතරෙන් තුනක් පාලනය කළ අමාත්‍යාංශ භාර ගත් අතර මානව හිමිකම් උල්ලංඝනය කිරීම් සහ දූෂණ චෝදනා නොතකා ජනතා සහයෝගය ගොඩනඟා ගත්හ. නමුත් 2019 වන විට, ගෝඨාභය ජනාධිපති වූ විට, දකුණු ආසියාවේ වඩාත්ම සංවර්ධිත ජාතිය විනාශයට ගෙන යන අන්තර් ගැටුම් සහ අක්‍රියතාවයෙන් පවුල විනාශ විය.

සම්මුඛ සාකච්ඡා වලදී වත්මන් සහ හිටපු ඇමතිවරුන්, විදේශ රාජ්‍ය තාන්ත්‍රිකයින් සහ රාජපක්ෂ විශ්වාසවන්තයින් ඇතැමුන් මුල්වරට කතා කළේ පවුල දෙකඩ වන අයුරු දැකීමෙන් බව ගෝඨාභය සහ මහින්ද ඇතුළු ඔවුන්ගේ කණ්ඩායම් හැප්පුනේ ඇමති පත්වීම් හා කෘෂිකර්ම ප්‍රතිපත්ති, ආයෝජන ගනුදෙනු සහ දේශපාලන වාසි. මේ වසරේ ආර්ථිකය නිදහස් කඩාවැටීමකට ලක් වූ විට, රාජපක්ෂ ගෝලයන් කිහිප දෙනෙකුගේ සහාය ඇතිව මහින්ද, ඔහු පසෙකට වී සිටිය යුතුය යන ගෝඨාභයගේ කැමැත්තට විරුද්ධ විය.

මැයි 9 වැනිදා මහින්දගේ පරිශ්‍රය වටලා සිටි මහින්දගේ අභ්‍යන්තර කවයේ සාමාජිකයන්ට ජනාධිපතිවරයා තමන්ව අතහැර දමා ඇතැයි හැඟෙන තරමට අවිශ්වාසය ගැඹුරු විය. අගමැති සමඟ සිටි ඥාති සොහොයුරෙකු වන උදයංග වීරතුංග සහ එහි සිටි තවත් පවුලේ සහයකයෙකු වොෂින්ටන් පෝස්ට් වෙත පැවසුවේ හමුදාවේ සිටින ගෝඨාභයගේ ආධාරකරුවන් හිතාමතාම ඔවුන්ගේ උදව්වට පැමිණීම පැය හයක් ප්‍රමාද කළ බවට තමන් සැක කරන බවයි.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාව සතුව ඩොලර් මිලියනයකට වඩා අඩු විදේශ සංචිතයක් ඇති බවත්, වෛද්‍ය සැපයුම් අඩුවෙමින් පවතින බවත්, ඉන්ධන නොමැති බවත් මේ සතියේ හෙළි කළ ගෝඨාභය තම සහෝදරයා වෙනුවට නව අගමැතිවරයකු පත් කිරීමෙන් පසු බලයේ එල්ලී සිටී.
ශ්‍රී ලංකාව “සම්පූර්ණ විනාශයකට” මුහුන දී සිටින බව හිටපු ජනාධිපති මෛත්‍රීපාල සිරිසේන මහතා පැවසීය. රාජවංශ දේශපාලනය ගැන රට පාඩමක් ඉගෙන ගෙන ඇත.

ක්‍රියාකාරී දේශපාලනයේ යෙදී සිටි ධනවත් හාල් පොල් ගොවියෙකුගේ පුත්‍රයෙකු වූ මහින්ද 1970 දී පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට ඉදිරිපත් වන විට ඔහු අනුගමනය කළේ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ ආධිපත්‍යය දරන ප්‍රභූ පවුල් කිහිපයක සම්ප්‍රදායයි. ඉන්දියාව.

සිංගප්පූරුවේ ජාතික විශ්ව විද්‍යාලයේ මහාචාර්ය රසීන් සාලි පැවසුවේ “ඔබ ස්ථාපිත පවුලකින් නොවේ නම් ඔබට [දේශපාලනය තුළ] ජයග්‍රහණය කළ නොහැක. "එබැවින් පද්ධතිය රාජ්‍යය කොල්ලකෑමට හැකි ස්ථාපිත අභ්‍යන්තරිකයින්ට ඉතිරිව ඇත."

දරුවන් 9 දෙනාගෙන් දෙවැන්නා වූ මහින්ද ආකර්ශනීය, ජනකායට ආදරය කළ අතර පවුලේ දේශපාලන උපායමාර්ගිකයා ලෙස සැලකෙන ඔහුගේ බාල සොහොයුරා වන බැසිල්ට සමීප විය. ඔවුන්ගේ මැද සොහොයුරා වන ගෝඨාභය සෑම විටම වෙනස් විය: හුදකලා, දේශපාලනික වශයෙන් අද්දැකීම් අඩු, ටීටෝටේලර් සහ නිර්මාංශිකයෙකු වූ ඔහු හමුදාවේ වසර 21 ක් ගත කළේය. “එයා පාරම්පරික ගෙදර එන්නේ අවුරුදු කාලෙට විතරයි” මහින්දගේ හිතවත් ඔවුන්ගේ ඥාති සොහොයුරා වීරතුංග සිහිපත් කළේය.

2005 සිට මහින්දගේ 10 වසරක ජනාධිපති ධුර කාලය තුළ රාජපක්ෂලා පවුලේ ව්‍යාපාරයක් ලෙස රට ගෙන ගියහ. ඔහු ගෝඨාභය ආරක්ෂක ලේකම් ලෙස නම් කරන ලද අතර බැසිල් සහ ඔවුන්ගේ වැඩිමහල් සහෝදරයා වන චමල් වාරිමාර්ග හා ආර්ථික සංවර්ධනය භාරව සිටියහ. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වසර ගණනාවක වර්ධනයක් භුක්ති වින්දා, විදේශ ණය කන්දකින් පෝෂණය විය.
දෙමල කැරලිකරුවන්ට එරෙහි 26 වසරක සිවිල් යුද්ධයේ ලේ වැකි නමුත් තීරණාත්මක ජයග්‍රහණය සහ සිංහල බෞද්ධ ජාතිකවාදයට ඔහු නිතර කළ ආයාචනා කිරීම අනුමත කළ ඡන්දදායකයින්ගේ ප්‍රශංසාවෙන් මහින්ද සතුටට පත් විය.

එහෙත් චීන රාජ්‍ය සමාගම් හා නිලධාරීන් සමඟ ප්‍රශ්නගත ගනුදෙනු ඇතුළු දූෂණ චෝදනා මහින්ද වටා කැරකෙන්නට විය. 2006 දී යුක්රේනයෙන් මිග් ප්‍රහාරක යානා මිලදී ගැනීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් ගෝඨාභය ද සුළු ප්‍රමාණයකට සම්බන්ධ වූ අතර විමර්ශනයට මුහුණ දුන්නේය.

ට්‍රාන්ස්පේරන්සි ඉන්ටර්නැෂනල් ශ්‍රී ලංකා ආයතනයේ නියෝජ්‍ය විධායක අධ්‍යක්ෂ සංඛිත ගුණරත්න පැවසුවේ, මහින්දට සහ බැසිල්ට සුනාමි සහනාධාර වෙනතකට යොමු කිරීම, ඉඩම් මිලදී ගැනීමට මහජන මුදල් යෙදවීම ඇතුළු චෝදනා රැසකට මුහුණ දී ඇති නමුත් බොහෝ නඩු ඇණහිට ඇති බව හෝ ඉල්ලා අස්කර ගෙන ඇති බවයි. "රාජපක්‍ෂ දූෂණ චෝදනා බොහෝ මිනිසුන්ට සෙවණ දෙන මහා ගසක් වැනිය," ඇය පැවසුවාය.
2021 දී, පැන්ඩෝරා පේපර්ස් නමින් හැඳින්වෙන මූල්‍ය ලේඛන පිටාර ගැලීමෙන් හෙළි වූයේ රාජපක්ෂ සහෝදරයන්ගේ ලේලියක ඩොලර් මිලියන ගණනක් අක්වෙරළ ගිණුම්වල සඟවා ඇති බවයි.

රාජපක්ෂලාගේ යැයි කියන ගජමිතුරුකම් සහ දූෂණය ගැන වැඩෙන කෝපය මධ්‍යයේ, 2015 දී මහින්ද තෙවැනි වාරය සඳහා වූ ලංසුවකින් පරාජයට පත් විය. වහාම වාගේ බටහිර ගැති ව්‍යාපාරික විධායකයින්ගේ, මිලිටරි දැඩි මතධාරීන්ගේ සහ බෞද්ධ භික්ෂූන්ගේ සාරාංශික සන්ධානයක් නව අපේක්ෂකයෙකු හඳුනා ගත්හ: ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ .

නව දේශපාලන අනුග්‍රාහකයන්ගේ සහාය ඇතිව ගෝඨාභය මහින්ද සමඟ ගැටෙන බව ඉක්මනින්ම පෙනෙන්නට විය. පිරිමින් එකිනෙකාට සෘජුව මුහුණ දුන්නේ කලාතුරකිනි, නමුත් ඉහළ දේශපාලන ක්‍රීඩා සහ සුළු දූෂණ ඇතුළු සෑම දෙයකටම ඔවුන් එකඟ නොවූ බව පවුලේ විශ්වාසවන්තයින් පැවසීය.

ගෝඨාභයගේ අපේක්ෂකත්වය දියත් කළ බවට පුලුල් ලෙස සැලකෙන මාධ්‍ය ප්‍රධානියෙකු වන දිලිත් ජයවීරට 2018 දී මහින්ද විසින් ගෝඨාභයගේ නිවසට කැඳවා ගත් සිදුවීමක් මතකයි. දේශපාලන හිතවතෙකු වූ ප්‍රබල හිමිනමකට නොමිලේ විදුලිය ලබා ගත හැකි වන පරිදි නීති විරෝධී ලෙස ඉදිකරන ලද නිවාඩු නිකේතනයකට මහින්ද ගෝඨාභයගේ නම දමා තිබුණි. අපකීර්තිය කාන්දු වීමට ආසන්න වූ අතර, බොහෝ විට සිදු වූ පරිදි, මහින්ද තම සහෝදරයාට පැවසීමට මැලි වූ නිසා, ඔහු ඔහුට ආරංචිය කියන්නට ජයවීරට තල්ලු කළේය.
ගෝඨාබය “කෝපයෙන්” සිටි බවත්, තම සහෝදරයා සමඟ මෝටර් රථයක් බෙදා ගැනීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරමින් බෞද්ධ විහාරස්ථානයකට කඩා වැදුණු බවත් ජයවීර පැවසීය.

2018 ඔක්තෝම්බර් මාසයේදී, එවකට ජනාධිපතිව සිටි සිරිසේන, ඔහුගේ අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ නෙරපා හැර, ඔහු වසර තුනකට පෙර පැවති මැතිවරණයේදී ඔහු පරාජය කළ මහින්ද වෙනුවට පත් කිරීමත් සමඟ ව්‍යවස්ථාමය අර්බුදයක් පැන නැගුනි. දෙදෙනාම රටේ අංක 2 රැකියාවට හිමිකම් කීම නිසා අගනුවර නොසන්සුන් වූ අතර වික්‍රමසිංහ බලහත්කාරයෙන් ඉවත් කළ හැකි බවට කටකතා පැතිර ගියේය.
මහින්ද සහ බැසිල් තමන්ව අභිබවා යලිත් බලයට ඒමට උත්සාහ කරන බවට බියෙන් ගෝඨාභය රහසේ වික්‍රමසිංහ හමුවී ඔහුට සහය දීමට පොරොන්දු විය.
වැඩි කල් යන්නට මත්තෙන්, ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය මහින්දගේ ඉල්ලීමට එරෙහිව තීන්දුවක් දුන් අතර, ඔහු පසුබැස්සේය. පවුලේ අයට ගෝඨාභයට සහයෝගය දීම හැර වෙනත් විකල්පයක් තිබුණේ නැත.

මැතිවරණයට පෙරාතුව, මුස්ලිම් අන්තවාදීන්ගේ ත්‍රස්තවාදී ප්‍රහාර ශ්‍රී ලංකාව දෙදරා ගිය අතර, හිටපු හමුදා නිලධාරියා වටා සිංහල බෞද්ධ සහයෝගය ශක්තිමත් විය. මැතිවරණ ප්‍රචාරක මාවතේ දී ගෝඨාභය ආරක්‍ෂාව, යහපාලනය සහ සංවර්ධනය ගැන කතා කළ අතර, තමන් තාක්‍ෂණ ශිල්පියකු ලෙස පෙනී සිටිමින්, මීළඟ සිංගප්පූරුව ලෙස ඉන්දියාවේ සහ චීන ආධාර ලබන අහස උසට නැඟී එන අහස උසට නැඟෙන කොළඹ සමඟ කොළඹ ගැන කතා කළේය. ඔහු ජයග්‍රහණය කළේ විශාල ඡන්ද ප්‍රමාණයකින්.

2019 නොවැම්බර් 19 වෙනිදා දිවුරුම් දෙන දිනයේ ගෝඨාභය සිය පවුලෙන් වෙන්වීමක් සංඥා කළේය. ඔහු රාජපක්ෂ වංශයේ අත්සන සහිත රතු පැහැති "සාටක" පැළඳීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කර, කොට අත් සහිත කමිසයකට කැමති විය. මහින්ද ජනාධිපතිව සිටියදී රුපියල් දහසේ නෝට්ටුවල තමාගේම රුව මුද්‍රණය කළ මෙන් නොව, ගෝඨාභය රජයේ කාර්යාලවල ඔහුගේ නිල රුව එල්ලීම තහනම් කළේය.
නමුත් පසුදා “පල්ලමේ ආරම්භය” යැයි පසුව ගෝඨාභයගේ ජනමාධ්‍ය අමාත්‍යවරයා වූ හිටපු මූල්‍ය විධායකයෙකු වූ නාලක ගොඩහේවා පැවසීය.

ගෝඨාභයගේ බටහිර ගැති ව්‍යාපාරික අංශයේ ආධාරකරුවන් පත්වීම් ලැයිස්තුවක් නිර්දේශ කර තිබුණත්, ජනාධිපතිවරයා සිය පළමු කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලය එළිදක්වන විට එය අගමැති ලෙස මහින්ද විසින් මෙහෙයවන ලද අතර එය බැසිල් සහ මහින්ද හිතවාදීන්ගෙන් පිරී ගියේය. ඔවුන් දැඩි බදු කප්පාදුවක් ක්‍රියාත්මක කළ අතර නය වැඩිවීම නොතකා ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලෙන් ආධාර ලබා ගැනීමට එරෙහිව තර්ක කළහ. ගෝලීය ආහාර මිල ඉහළ ගිය ආකාරයටම, භෝග අස්වැන්නට හානි කරන රසායනික පොහොර තහනමකට ගෝඨාභය පුද්ගලිකව තල්ලු කළේය.

මහින්දගේ හිතවතුන් කීවේ තමන් කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලය හැදුවේ ගෝඨාභයගේ පත්කිරීම්වලින් හෑල්ලූ වීමට පමණක් බවයි. අවස්ථා කිහිපයකදීම, රජය විසින් පැය 24ක් තුළ වෙළඳ ප්‍රතිපත්ති නිකුත් කළ අතර ඒවා ඉවත් කර ගන්නා ලදී. “ඔබලාට සිටියේ ලේකම්වරුන් සමඟ සටන් කරන ඇමතිවරු” වීරතුංග පැවසීය. "සටන් පරිපාලනය පුරා පැතිර ගියේය."
රාජපක්ෂලා එක ප්‍රශ්නයක් මත එක්සත් විය: 2020 දී සම්මත කරන ලද ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනයක් දූෂණ විමර්ශන කොමිෂන් සභා දුර්වල කළ අතර ජනාධිපතිවරයාට අධිකරණය සම්බන්ධයෙන් දුරදිග යන බලතල ලබා දුන්නේය.
2022 මුල් භාගය වන විට ආර්ථිකය නිදහස් වැටීමකට ලක් විය. සහල් වැනි ආහාර ද්‍රව්‍යවල මිල පෙර වසරකට වඩා දෙගුණයකින් වැඩි විය. පෙට්‍රල් සහ විදුලිය හිඟ විය. විදේශ මුදල් සංචිත අවසන් විය.

අප්‍රේල් මාසයේදී, රාජපක්ෂලා දේශපාලනයෙන් ඉවත් විය යුතු බවට බලකරමින් අගනුවර රාත්‍රී පෙළපාලි මුල් බැස ගත් අතර ඇතැමුන් ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයට පත් විය. ගෝඨාභයගේ මුළු කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලයම - එයට බැසිල්, මුදල් ඇමති; වැඩිමහල් සහෝදරයා, චමල්; සහ මහින්දගේ පුතා - ගෝඨාභයට නව රජයක් පිහිටුවීමට අවස්ථාවක් ලබා දෙමින් ඉල්ලා අස්විය. විදේශ ණය දෙන්නන් වෙත ඉදිරිපත් කිරීමට සහ හදිසි ඇපදීමක් සඳහා සාකච්ඡා කිරීමට ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට ස්ථාවර ප්‍රතිරූපයක් අවශ්‍ය විය.
එහෙත් අගමැති මහින්ද විපක්ෂයේ ඉල්ලීම්වලට විරුද්ධ වූ අතර ජනාධිපතිවරයාගෙන් ඉල්ලා අස්වන ලෙසට පවා සංඥා කළේය.

ගෝඨාභය බල කළේ නැහැ. “ජී.ආර්. ‘මට අවශ්‍ය දේ ඔහු දන්නවා’ යැයි කියනු ඇත, ”රාජපක්ෂවරුන් කිහිප දෙනෙකු නික්ම ගිය පසු කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලයට එක් වූ ගොඩහේවා පැවසීය. "බැසිල්ගේ සහ මහින්දගේ සහයෝගය ඔහුට අවශ්‍ය බව ඔහුට හැඟුණා."

මහින්දට දැඩි පීඩනයක් එල්ල වීමත් සමඟ ඔහුගේ ආධාරකරුවන් මැයි 9 වැනිදා අගමැතිගේ පරිශ්‍රය වන අරලියගහ මන්දිරයේ රැස්වීමක් සංවිධානය කළේය. පවුලේ අභ්‍යන්තරිකයින් දෙදෙනෙකුට සහ සිද්ධියේ වීඩියෝවලට අනුව, කුලදෙටුවන්, ධෛර්‍ය සම්පන්න බවක් සහ ඉල්ලා අස්වීමට උත්සාහ කරමින්, හදිසියේම ජවසම්පන්න වූ බවක් පෙනෙන්නට තිබුණි.

"ජනතාවට නිතරම ඇහුම්කන් දෙන නායකයෙකු ලෙස, මම දැන් ඔබෙන් අසමි: කළ යුත්තේ කුමක්ද?" මහින්ද කීවේ පහන් කූරු දැල්වූ ශාලාවක දෙපයින් වාඩි වී සිටි දහස් ගණන් ආධාරකරුවන්ටය. "ඔබ සිටිය යුතුයි!" සමූහයා ඝෝෂා කළහ. "ඒ කියන්නේ මම ඉල්ලා අස්විය යුතු නැද්ද?" ඔහු නැවත ඇසුවේ සටන් කිරීමට ඇමතුම් වල පොඟවමිනි.
රැලිය අවසන් වූ විට ආධාරකරුවන් වානේ දඬු සහ ලී කූරු රැගෙන අරලියගහ මන්දිරයෙන් පිටතට ගලා ආ අතර, රාජ්‍ය විරෝධී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට පහර දෙමින් සහ ජාතිය කම්පනයට පත් කරන ලද ලේ වැකි ප්‍රතිචාරයක් ඇති කළේය.

තමාට නවතින ලෙස බල කළ පුතුන් සමඟ අරලියගහ මන්දිරයට වී සිටි මහින්ද සවස 4ට සිය කථිකයාට කීවේය. ඉල්ලා අස්වෙනවා කියලා. කථිකයා මාධ්‍ය වෙත ප්‍රවෘත්තිය ප්‍රචාරය කළ නමුත් එය ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය නැවැත්වූයේ නැත, පරිශ්‍රයේ සිටි පුද්ගලයින් දෙදෙනෙකු පැවසීය.
පවුලේ අයගේ ආයාචනා නොතකා, විරෝධතාකරුවන් ඒ වන විටත් ගේට්ටුවක් කැඩීමෙන් පසු, හමුදාව රාත්‍රී 11.00 දක්වා ශක්තිමත් කිරීම් එවා නැත. පාන්දර 4 ට මහින්ද හමුදා කඳවුරකට සොල්දාදුවන් විසින් ඉවත් කරන ලදී.

“මෙම ඇනහිටීම හිතාමතා කළ දෙයක් බව මහින්දට වැටහුණා,” යැයි වීරතුංග කීවේ ගෝඨාභය තම සහෝදරයන් බිය ගැන්වීමට උත්සාහ කරන බවට චෝදනා කළේය. එහෙත් එදින ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ පැත්තේ සිටි ඇමැතිවරු දෙදෙනෙක් කියා සිටියේ ඔහු කෝපයෙන් හමුදා නිලධාරීන්ට කතා කළ බවකි.
“ඔහුට හමුදාව හෝ පොලිසිය පාලනය කළ නොහැකි විය,” ගොඩහේවා පැවසීය.
ගොඩහේවා සහ විදේශ රාජ්‍ය තාන්ත්‍රිකයින් පැවසුවේ බටහිර නිලධාරීන් සමඟ නිතර සම්බන්ධකම් පවත්වන හමුදාපති ශවේන්ද්‍ර සිල්වා හමුදා මර්දනයකට අණ දෙනු ඇතැයි යන බිය නිසා තම හමුදා යෙදවීමට මැලි වූ බවයි.

එදින හමුදාව නොපැමිණීම සහෝදරයන් අතර විරසකය පුළුල් කළේය. මේ සතියේ පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට කළ කතාවකදී චමල් 2015 දී දේශපාලනයෙන් ඉවත් නොවීම ගැන මහින්දට දොස් කීවේය. මෑතක පැවති රාජපක්ෂලාගේ පාක්ෂිකයින්ගේ රැස්වීමකදී පවුලේ හිතවතුන් කෝපයෙන් විමසා සිටියේ මැයි 9 වැනි දින ඔවුන්ව ආරක්ෂා නොකළේ මන්ද යන්නයි. “ගෝඨාබය දැන් පක්ෂයට සලකන ආකාරය ජනතා කෝපයේ දිශාව තීරණය කරනු ඇත,” වීරතුංග පැවසීය.
මැයි 12 දා, අවුල් සහගත සහ හුදකලා වූ ගෝඨාභය නව අගමැතිවරයකු නම් කළේය: රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ, 2018 දී ඔහු තම සහෝදරයාට එරෙහි ස්ථාවරය සඳහා ප්‍රථම වරට විහිළු කළ විට ඔහුට රහසිගතව මුණගැසුණු පුද්ගලයා.

වසර හතරකට පසු, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ බලවත්ම පවුල බිඳ වැටෙමින් තිබුණි - සමහර විට යහපත සඳහා යැයි මාධ්‍ය අධිපති ජයවීර පැවසීය. "රාජපක්ෂලා සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව ඛේදවාචකයකින් අවසන් විය," ඔහු පැවසීය. "එය අවසන් වූයේ ඔවුන්ගේම ක්‍රියාවන් නිසාය."

ta.title: ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபயவும் அவரது முன்னாள் பிரதமர் மஹிந்தவும் ஒருவரையொருவர் நேரடியாக எதிர்கொள்வது அரிதாகவே இருந்தது, இருப்பினும் அவர்கள் அதிக அரசியல் விளையாட்டு மற்றும் சிறிய ஊழல் உட்பட அனைத்திலும் உடன்படவில்லை.
body_lines: சிறிலங்கா பிரதமரின் உத்தியோகபூர்வ இல்லத்தின் வாயில்கள் மீது கும்பல் தாக்கியது, அதன் அளவு மற்றும் சீற்றம் ஆபத்தானது.

பல வாரங்களாக, 76 வயதான பிரதமர் மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச, பொருளாதாரம் சீர்குலைந்து, எதிர்ப்புகள் வெடித்ததால், பதவி விலக அழுத்தம் கொடுக்கப்பட்டது. ஜனாதிபதியின் சகோதரரும், அவரது சொந்த அரசியல் பரம்பரையின் தலைவருமான மகிந்த ஒரு காலத்தில் அப்பாச்சி, மக்களின் அன்புக்குரிய தந்தை எனப் போற்றப்பட்டார். இப்போது அவர் தனது இரண்டாவது மாடி படுக்கையறையில் பதுங்கியிருந்தார், உறவினர்களுடன் சேர்ந்து இராணுவ அதிகாரிகளை ஆவேசமாக அழைத்து, மீட்குமாறு கெஞ்சினார்.

வாயில்களுக்கு வெளியே, மகிந்தவின் ஆதரவாளர்களால் தாக்கப்பட்ட அரசாங்க எதிர்ப்புப் போராட்டக்காரர்கள் தங்கள் பழிவாங்கும் நடவடிக்கைகளில் ஈடுபட்டுள்ளனர் - கலவரம், பேருந்துகள் எரிப்பு மற்றும் ராஜபக்ஷக்களின் கூட்டாளிகளுக்கு சொந்தமான நூற்றுக்கணக்கான வீடுகளுக்கு தீ வைத்தனர். அவர்களது கட்சியைச் சேர்ந்த ஒரு சட்டமன்ற உறுப்பினர் அடித்துக் கொல்லப்பட்டார், அவரது உடல் தெருக்களில் இழுத்துச் செல்லப்பட்டது.
அந்த நாள், மே 9, இலங்கையின் சமீபத்திய வரலாற்றில் மிகவும் வன்முறை மற்றும் குழப்பமான நாளாகும். ஆனால் அது ராஜபக்சேவின் வீட்டில் பல ஆண்டுகளாக கொந்தளிப்பு ஏற்பட்டது.

கடந்த 20 ஆண்டுகளாக ராஜபக்சே சகோதரர்கள் இங்கு அரசியலில் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தி வருகின்றனர். 2005 இல் மஹிந்தவை ஜனாதிபதியாக வெற்றியடையச் செய்த பின்னர், அவரது சகோதரர்களான சமல், கோத்தபாய மற்றும் பசில் ஆகியோர் தேசிய வரவு செலவுத் திட்டத்தில் முக்கால்வாசிப் பகுதியைக் கட்டுப்படுத்திய அமைச்சுக்களைக் கைப்பற்றினர் மற்றும் மனித உரிமை மீறல்கள் மற்றும் ஊழல் குற்றச்சாட்டுக்கள் இருந்தபோதிலும் மக்கள் ஆதரவை உருவாக்கினர். ஆனால் 2019 ஆம் ஆண்டளவில், கோட்டாபய ஜனாதிபதியானபோது, ​​தெற்காசியாவின் மிகவும் வளர்ந்த தேசத்தை அழிவுக்கு இட்டுச் செல்லும் உட்பூசல் மற்றும் செயலிழப்பு ஆகியவற்றால் குடும்பம் சிதைந்தது.

நேர்காணல்களில், தற்போதைய மற்றும் முன்னாள் அமைச்சர்கள், வெளிநாட்டு இராஜதந்திரிகள் மற்றும் ராஜபக்சவின் நம்பிக்கைக்குரியவர்கள், அவர்களில் சிலர் குடும்பம் பிளவுபட்டதைக் கண்டு முதல் முறையாக பேசினர் என்று கோட்டாபய மற்றும் மகிந்த மற்றும் அந்தந்த அணியினர் அமைச்சர் நியமனங்கள் மற்றும் விவசாயக் கொள்கைகள், முதலீட்டு ஒப்பந்தங்கள் மற்றும் மோதினர். அரசியல் உதவிகள். இந்த ஆண்டு பொருளாதாரம் வீழ்ச்சியடைந்த நிலையில், பல ராஜபக்ச மைந்தர்களின் ஆதரவுடன் மகிந்த, கோட்டாபயவின் விருப்பத்தை எதிர்த்தார்.

மே 9 ஆம் திகதி மகிந்தவின் வளாகத்தை முற்றுகையிட்ட மஹிந்தவின் உள்வட்ட உறுப்பினர்கள், ஜனாதிபதி தம்மைக் கைவிட்டதாக உணரும் அளவிற்கு அவநம்பிக்கை ஆழமடைந்தது. பிரதமருடன் இருந்த உறவினரான உதயங்க வீரதுங்கவும், அங்கிருந்த மற்றொரு குடும்ப உதவியாளரும், கோட்டாபயவின் இராணுவத்தில் உள்ள ஆதரவாளர்கள் வேண்டுமென்றே தங்களுக்கு உதவிக்கு வருவதற்கு ஆறு மணித்தியாலங்கள் தாமதித்ததாக சந்தேகிப்பதாக வாஷிங்டன் போஸ்ட்டிடம் தெரிவித்தனர்.

கோத்தபய தனது சகோதரருக்குப் பதிலாக ஒரு புதிய பிரதமரை நியமித்த பின்னர் அதிகாரத்தில் ஒட்டிக்கொண்டார், அவர் இந்த வாரம் இலங்கையில் $1 மில்லியனுக்கும் குறைவான வெளிநாட்டு கையிருப்பு உள்ளது, மருத்துவப் பொருட்கள் குறைந்து வருதல் மற்றும் கிட்டத்தட்ட எரிபொருள் இல்லை என்பதை வெளிப்படுத்தினார்.
இலங்கை "முழு அழிவை எதிர்நோக்கியுள்ளது" என முன்னாள் ஜனாதிபதி மைத்திரிபால சிறிசேன தெரிவித்துள்ளார். வம்ச அரசியலைப் பற்றி நாடு பாடம் கற்றுக்கொண்டது.

1970 ஆம் ஆண்டு அரசியலில் ஈடுபட்டிருந்த ஒரு பணக்கார அரிசி மற்றும் தென்னை விவசாயியின் மகனான மகிந்த, 1970 இல் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்கு போட்டியிட்ட போது, ​​இலங்கையின் கரையோரத்தில் ஒரு பசுமையான கண்ணீர்த் துளி வடிவிலான தீவில் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தும் சில உயரடுக்கு குடும்பங்களின் பாரம்பரியத்தைப் பின்பற்றினார். இந்தியா.

சிங்கப்பூர் தேசிய பல்கலைக்கழகத்தின் பேராசிரியரான ரஸீன் சாலி, “நீங்கள் நிறுவப்பட்ட குடும்பத்தைச் சேர்ந்தவராக இல்லாவிட்டால் [அரசியலில்] வெற்றி பெற முடியாது. "எனவே இந்த அமைப்பு அரசைக் கொள்ளையடிக்கக்கூடிய உள்நாட்டினருக்கு விடப்பட்டுள்ளது."

ஒன்பது பிள்ளைகளில் இரண்டாவது குழந்தை, மகிந்த கவர்ச்சியானவர், கூட்டத்தை நேசித்தார் மற்றும் குடும்பத்தின் அரசியல் மூலோபாயவாதியாகக் கருதப்படும் அவரது இளைய சகோதரர் பசிலுடன் நெருக்கமாக இருந்தார். அவர்களின் நடுத்தர சகோதரரான கோட்டாபய எப்போதும் வித்தியாசமானவர்: ஒதுங்கியவர், அரசியல் அனுபவமில்லாதவர், 21 வருடங்கள் இராணுவத்தில் பணியாற்றியவர் மற்றும் சைவ உணவு உண்பவர். புத்தாண்டின் போது மாத்திரமே அவர் மூதாதையரின் வீட்டிற்குச் செல்வார் என மஹிந்தவுக்கு நெருக்கமான அவர்களது உறவினரான வீரதுங்க நினைவு கூர்ந்தார்.

2005 இல் தொடங்கி மஹிந்தவின் 10 வருட ஜனாதிபதி காலத்தில் ராஜபக்சக்கள் குடும்ப வியாபாரம் போன்று நாட்டை நடத்தினார்கள். அவர் கோட்டாபயவின் பாதுகாப்பு செயலாளராக நியமிக்கப்பட்டார், அதே நேரத்தில் பசிலும் அவர்களது மூத்த சகோதரர் சமலும் நீர்ப்பாசனம் மற்றும் பொருளாதார அபிவிருத்திக்கு பொறுப்பாக இருந்தனர். மலையளவு வெளிநாட்டுக் கடனினால் தூண்டப்பட்ட பல வருடகால வளர்ச்சியை இலங்கை அனுபவித்தது.

தமிழ் கிளர்ச்சியாளர்களுக்கு எதிரான 26 ஆண்டுகால உள்நாட்டுப் போரில் தனது இரத்தம் தோய்ந்த ஆனால் தீர்க்கமான வெற்றியை அங்கீகரித்த மகிந்த வாக்காளர்களின் பாராட்டுக்களையும், சிங்கள பௌத்த தேசியவாதத்திற்கு அடிக்கடி அழைப்பு விடுத்ததையும் அங்கீகரித்தார்.

ஆனால், சீன அரச நிறுவனங்கள் மற்றும் அதிகாரிகளுடனான கேள்விக்குரிய ஒப்பந்தங்கள் உட்பட ஊழல் குற்றச்சாட்டுகள் மஹிந்தவைச் சுற்றியே சுழன்றன. கோத்தபயவும் குறைந்த அளவிலேயே சிக்கியிருந்தார், மேலும் 2006 ஆம் ஆண்டு உக்ரேனிலிருந்து MiG போர் விமானங்களை வாங்கியது தொடர்பாக ஆய்வுகளை எதிர்கொண்டார்.

சுனாமி நிவாரண உதவிகளை திருப்பியளித்தமை மற்றும் காணிகளை கொள்வனவு செய்வதற்கு பொது நிதியை பயன்படுத்தியமை உட்பட பல குற்றச்சாட்டுகளை மஹிந்த மற்றும் பசில் எதிர்கொண்டுள்ள போதிலும், பல வழக்குகள் ஸ்தம்பிதமடைந்துள்ளன அல்லது மீளப்பெறப்பட்டதாக டிரான்ஸ்பரன்சி இன்டர்நேஷனல் ஸ்ரீலங்காவின் பிரதி நிறைவேற்றுப் பணிப்பாளர் சங்கித்த குணரத்ன தெரிவித்தார். ராஜபக்சவின் ஊழல் என்பது பலருக்கு நிழல் தரும் பெரிய மரம் போன்றது என்றும் அவர் கூறினார்.
2021 ஆம் ஆண்டில், பண்டோரா பேப்பர்ஸ் எனப்படும் நிதி ஆவணங்களின் கசிவு, ராஜபக்ச சகோதரர்களின் மருமகள் வெளிநாட்டுக் கணக்குகளில் மில்லியன் கணக்கான டாலர்களை மறைத்து வைத்திருப்பதை வெளிப்படுத்தியது.

ராஜபக்சக்களின் குற்றச் சாட்டுக்கள் மற்றும் ஊழல்கள் மீதான பெருகிவரும் கோபத்தின் மத்தியில், 2015 இல் மஹிந்த மூன்றாவது தவணைக்கான முயற்சியில் தோல்வியடைந்தார். ஏறக்குறைய உடனடியாக, மேற்கத்திய சார்பு வர்த்தக நிர்வாகிகள், இராணுவ கடும்போக்காளர்கள் மற்றும் பௌத்த பிக்குகள் அடங்கிய ஒரு தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்ட கூட்டணி புதிய வேட்பாளரை அடையாளம் காட்டியது: கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச .

புதிய அரசியல் அனுசரணையாளர்களின் ஆதரவுடன் கோட்டாபய மஹிந்தவுடன் மோதுவார் என்பது விரைவில் வெளிப்பட்டது. ஆண்கள் ஒருவரையொருவர் நேரடியாக எதிர்கொள்வது அரிது, இருப்பினும் அவர்கள் அதிக அரசியல் விளையாட்டு மற்றும் சிறிய ஊழல் உட்பட அனைத்திலும் உடன்படவில்லை என்று குடும்ப நம்பிக்கையாளர்கள் தெரிவித்தனர்.

கோட்டாபயவின் வேட்புமனுவை அறிமுகப்படுத்தியதன் மூலம் பரவலாக அறியப்படும் ஊடகவியலாளர் திலித் ஜயவீர, 2018 ஆம் ஆண்டு மஹிந்தவினால் கோட்டாபயவின் வீட்டுக்கு அழைக்கப்பட்ட சம்பவத்தை நினைவு கூர்ந்தார். அரசியல் கூட்டாளியான பலம் வாய்ந்த பிக்கு ஒருவருக்கு இலவச மின்சாரம் கிடைக்க வேண்டும் என்பதற்காக, சட்டவிரோதமாக கட்டப்பட்ட உல்லாச விடுதிக்கு கோட்டாபயவின் பெயரை மகிந்த போட்டிருந்தார். இந்த ஊழல் கசியவிடப் போகிறது, அடிக்கடி நடப்பது போல, மகிந்த தனது சகோதரரிடம் சொல்லத் தயங்கினார், எனவே அவர் இந்தச் செய்தியை அவருக்குத் தெரியப்படுத்த ஜெயவீரவைத் தள்ளினார்.
கோத்தபய "கோபமடைந்தார்" என்று ஜெயவீர கூறினார், மேலும் தனது சகோதரருடன் காரைப் பகிர்ந்து கொள்ள மறுத்து புத்த கோவிலுக்குச் சென்றார்.

2018 அக்டோபரில், அப்போதைய ஜனாதிபதியாக இருந்த சிறிசேன, தனது பிரதமரான ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவை பதவி நீக்கம் செய்து, அவருக்குப் பதிலாக மகிந்தவை நியமித்தபோது ஒரு அரசியலமைப்பு நெருக்கடி வெடித்தது, அவர் மூன்று ஆண்டுகளுக்கு முன்பு தேர்தலில் தோற்கடித்தார். இருவருமே நாட்டின் நம்பர் 2 வேலையில் உரிமைகோருவதால் தலைநகரம் பதற்றமாக இருந்தது மற்றும் விக்கிரமசிங்க பலவந்தமாக அகற்றப்படலாம் என்ற வதந்திகள் பரவின.
மகிந்தவும் பசிலும் அவரைப் பின்னுக்குத் தள்ளிவிட்டு, தாங்கள் மீண்டும் ஆட்சிக்கு வருவார்கள் என்று அஞ்சி, கோட்டாபய, விக்ரமசிங்கவை ரகசியமாகச் சந்தித்து தனது ஆதரவை உறுதி செய்தார்.
விரைவில், உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் மஹிந்தவின் கோரிக்கைக்கு எதிராக தீர்ப்பளித்தது, அவர் பின்வாங்கினார். கோட்டாபயவை ஆதரிப்பதைத் தவிர குடும்பத்திற்கு வேறு வழியில்லை.

தேர்தலுக்கு முன்னதாக, முஸ்லீம் தீவிரவாதிகளின் பயங்கரவாதத் தாக்குதல்கள் இலங்கையை உலுக்கியது, முன்னாள் இராணுவ வீரரைச் சுற்றி சிங்கள பௌத்த ஆதரவைப் பெருக்கியது. பிரச்சாரப் பாதையில், கோத்தபய பாதுகாப்பு, நல்லாட்சி மற்றும் வளர்ச்சி, தன்னை ஒரு தொழில்நுட்ப வல்லுநராகக் காட்டிக் கொண்டார், மேலும் கொழும்பை அடுத்த சிங்கப்பூர் என்று இந்திய மற்றும் சீன நிதியுதவியுடன் கூடிய உயரமான கட்டிடங்களுடன் பேசினார். அவர் அமோக வெற்றி பெற்றார்.

நவம்பர் 19, 2019 அன்று அவர் பதவியேற்ற நாளில், கோட்டாபய தனது குடும்பத்தில் இருந்து பிரிந்து செல்வதை அடையாளம் காட்டினார். அவர் ராஜபக்ச குலத்தின் கையொப்ப தாவணியான சிவப்பு "சதகா" அணிய மறுத்து, குட்டைக் கை சட்டையை அணிய மறுத்தார். ஜனாதிபதியாக இருந்த போது 1,000 ரூபாய் நோட்டுகளில் தனது சொந்த உருவத்தை அச்சடித்த மஹிந்த போல் அல்லாமல், கோட்டாபய தனது உத்தியோகபூர்வ உருவப்படத்தை அரசாங்க அலுவலகங்களில் தொங்கவிடுவதை தடை செய்தார்.
ஆனால் அடுத்த நாள் "வீழ்ச்சியின் ஆரம்பம்" என்று கோட்டாபயவின் ஊடகத்துறை அமைச்சராக இருந்த முன்னாள் நிதி நிர்வாகி நாலக கொடஹேவா கூறினார்.

கோட்டாபயவின் மேற்கத்திய சார்பு வணிகத் துறை ஆதரவாளர்கள் நியமனங்களின் பட்டியலைப் பரிந்துரைத்தனர், ஆனால் ஜனாதிபதி தனது முதல் அமைச்சரவையை வெளியிட்டபோது, ​​அது மகிந்த பிரதமராக வழிநடத்தப்பட்டது மற்றும் பசில் மற்றும் மகிந்த விசுவாசிகளுடன் சேமித்து வைக்கப்பட்டது. அவர்கள் செங்குத்தான வரி குறைப்புகளை இயற்றினர் மற்றும் பெருகிவரும் கடன் இருந்தபோதிலும் சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்திடம் இருந்து உதவி பெறுவதற்கு எதிராக வாதிட்டனர். உலகளாவிய உணவுப் பொருட்களின் விலைகள் உயர்ந்ததைப் போலவே பயிர் விளைச்சலைப் பாதிக்கும் இரசாயன உரங்கள் மீதான தடையை கோத்தபய தனிப்பட்ட முறையில் முன்வைத்தார்.

கோட்டாபயவின் நியமனங்கள் மூலம் தாங்கள் அமைச்சரவையை குழிபறிப்பதற்காகவே தாம் உருவாக்கியுள்ளதாக மஹிந்தவின் ஆதரவாளர்கள் தெரிவித்துள்ளனர். பல சந்தர்ப்பங்களில், அரசாங்கம் வர்த்தகக் கொள்கைகளை வெளியிட்டது, அவை 24 மணி நேரத்திற்குள் திரும்பப் பெறப்பட்டன. "உங்களிடம் அமைச்சர்கள், செயலாளர்களுடன் போராடினார்கள்" என்று வீரதுங்க கூறினார். "சண்டை நிர்வாகத்தில் ஊடுருவியது."

ராஜபக்சக்கள் ஒரு பிரச்சினையில் ஒன்றுபட்டனர்: 2020 இல் நிறைவேற்றப்பட்ட அரசியலமைப்புத் திருத்தம் ஊழலை விசாரிக்கும் ஆணைக்குழுக்களை பலவீனப்படுத்தியது மற்றும் நீதிமன்றங்கள் மீது ஜனாதிபதிக்கு நீண்டகால அதிகாரங்களை வழங்கியது.

2022 இன் தொடக்கத்தில், பொருளாதாரம் இலவச வீழ்ச்சியில் இருந்தது. அரிசி போன்ற உணவுப் பொருட்களின் விலை கடந்த ஆண்டை விட இருமடங்காக உயர்ந்துள்ளது. பெட்ரோல் மற்றும் மின்சாரம் பற்றாக்குறையாக இருந்தது. அந்நியச் செலாவணி கையிருப்பு தீர்ந்து கொண்டிருந்தது.

ஏப்ரலில், ராஜபக்சேக்கள் அரசியலை விட்டு வெளியேற வேண்டும் என்று கோரி தலைநகரில் இரவு ஆர்ப்பாட்டங்கள் நடந்தன, மேலும் சிலர் வன்முறையில் ஈடுபட்டனர். கோத்தபாயவின் முழு அமைச்சரவையும் - இதில் பசில், நிதி அமைச்சர்; மூத்த சகோதரர் சமல்; மற்றும் மஹிந்தவின் மகன் - ராஜினாமா செய்தார், புதிய அரசாங்கத்தை அமைப்பதற்கான வாய்ப்பை கோட்டாபயவுக்கு வழங்கினார். வெளிநாட்டு கடன் வழங்குபவர்களிடம் முன்வைப்பதற்கும், அவசர பிணை எடுப்பு குறித்து பேச்சுவார்த்தை நடத்துவதற்கும் இலங்கைக்கு ஒரு நிலையான படம் தேவைப்பட்டது.
ஆனால், பிரதமர் மஹிந்த, எதிர்க்கட்சிகளின் அழைப்புகளை எதிர்த்தார் மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதியிடமிருந்து விலகுவதற்கான சமிக்ஞைகளை கூட எதிர்த்தார்.

கோட்டாபய பிரச்சினையை வற்புறுத்தவில்லை. “ஜி.ஆர். ‘எனக்கு என்ன வேண்டும் என்பது அவருக்குத் தெரியும்’ என்று கூறுவார்,” என பல ராஜபக்சக்கள் வெளியேறிய பின்னர் அமைச்சரவையில் இணைந்த கொடஹேவா கூறினார். பசில் மற்றும் மகிந்தவின் ஆதரவு தனக்கு தேவை என அவர் உணர்ந்தார்.

மகிந்த மீது அழுத்தம் அதிகரித்துள்ள நிலையில், அவரது ஆதரவாளர்கள் மே 9 அன்று பிரதமரின் வளாகத்தில் உள்ள அலரிமாளிகையில் பேரணியை ஏற்பாடு செய்தனர். இரண்டு குடும்ப உள் நபர்கள் மற்றும் நிகழ்வின் வீடியோக்களின் படி, தேசபக்தர், உணர்ச்சிவசப்பட்டு, ராஜினாமா செய்ய நினைத்தார், திடீரென்று உற்சாகமடைந்தார்.

"எப்போதும் மக்களுக்கு செவிசாய்க்கும் ஒரு தலைவராக, நான் இப்போது உங்களிடம் கேட்கிறேன்: என்ன செய்ய வேண்டும்?" சரவிளக்கு ஏற்றப்பட்ட மண்டபத்தில் கால் மேல் கால் போட்டு அமர்ந்திருந்த ஆயிரக்கணக்கான ஆதரவாளர்களிடம் மஹிந்த கூறினார். "நீங்கள் தங்க வேண்டும்!" கூட்டம் அலைமோதியது. "நான் ராஜினாமா செய்யக்கூடாது என்று அர்த்தமா?" அவர் மீண்டும் கேட்டார், சண்டைக்கான அழைப்புகளில் நனைந்தார்.

பேரணி முடிந்ததும், ஆதரவாளர்கள் டெம்பிள் மரங்களிலிருந்து இரும்புக் கம்பிகள் மற்றும் மரக் குச்சிகளுடன் வெளியேறினர், அரசாங்க எதிர்ப்புப் போராட்டக்காரர்களை அடித்துத் தாக்கினர் மற்றும் தேசத்தை அதிர்ச்சிக்குள்ளாக்கிய இரத்தக்களரி பின்னடைவைத் தூண்டினர்.

தம்மை தங்கும்படி வற்புறுத்திய மகன்களுடன் அலரிமாளிகையில் தங்கியிருந்த மஹிந்த, மாலை 4 மணியளவில் தனது உரையாசிரியரிடம் தெரிவித்தார். அவர் ராஜினாமா செய்கிறார் என்று. உரையாசிரியர் செய்தியை ஊடகங்களுக்கு பரப்பினார், ஆனால் அது வன்முறையை நிறுத்தவில்லை என்று வளாகத்திற்குள் இருந்த இரண்டு பேர் தெரிவித்தனர்.

குடும்பத்தினரின் வேண்டுகோள்கள் இருந்தபோதிலும், எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் ஏற்கனவே ஒரு வாயிலை மீறிய பின்னர், இரவு 11 மணி வரை இராணுவம் வலுவூட்டல்களை அனுப்பவில்லை. அதிகாலை 4 மணியளவில் மகிந்த படையினரால் இராணுவ முகாமிற்கு வெளியேற்றப்பட்டார்.

கோட்டாபய தனது சகோதரர்களை அச்சுறுத்த முயற்சிப்பதாக குற்றம் சாட்டிய வீரதுங்க, “இந்த ஸ்தம்பிதம் வேண்டுமென்றே செய்யப்பட்டதை மஹிந்த புரிந்துகொண்டார். ஆனால், அன்றைய தினம் ஜனாதிபதியின் பக்கம் இருந்த இரண்டு அமைச்சர்கள், அவர் இராணுவ அதிகாரிகளை ஆவேசமாக அழைத்ததாகவும் பலனில்லை.
"அவரால் இராணுவத்தையோ அல்லது பொலிஸையோ கட்டுப்படுத்த முடியவில்லை," என்று கொடஹேவா கூறினார்.

கொடஹேவா மற்றும் வெளிநாட்டு இராஜதந்திரிகள், இராணுவத் தளபதி சவேந்திர சில்வா - மேற்கத்திய அதிகாரிகளுடன் அடிக்கடி தொடர்பில் இருந்தவர் - இராணுவ ஒடுக்குமுறைக்கு உத்தரவிடுவார் என்ற அச்சத்தில் தனது படைகளை நிலைநிறுத்தத் தயங்கினார்.

அன்றைய தினம் இராணுவம் இல்லாதது சகோதரர்களிடையே பிளவுகளை விரிவுபடுத்தியது. இந்த வாரம் பாராளுமன்றத்தில் ஆற்றிய உரையில், 2015ல் மகிந்த அரசியலை விட்டு வெளியேறவில்லை என்று சமல் சாடினார். மேலும் ராஜபக்சவின் கட்சி உறுப்பினர்களின் சமீபத்திய கூட்டத்தில், குடும்ப கூட்டாளிகள் கோபத்துடன் மே 9 அன்று முரண்பாடான ஒரு அரிய காட்சியில் ஏன் பாதுகாக்கப்படவில்லை என்று கேட்டனர். "கோட்டாபய இப்போது கட்சியை எப்படி நடத்துகிறார் என்பது மக்களின் கோபத்தின் திசையை தீர்மானிக்கும்" என்று வீரதுங்க கூறினார்.

மே 12 அன்று, குழப்பமான மற்றும் தனிமைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட கோத்தபயா ஒரு புதிய பிரதமரை நியமித்தார்: ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க, 2018 இல் அவர் தனது சகோதரருக்கு எதிரான நிலைப்பாட்டிற்காக முதன்முதலில் விளையாடியபோது அவர் இரகசியமாக சந்தித்தார்.

நான்கு ஆண்டுகளுக்குப் பிறகு, இலங்கையின் மிகவும் சக்திவாய்ந்த குடும்பம் சிதைந்தது - ஒருவேளை நல்லதாக இருக்கலாம் என்று ஊடக அதிபர் ஜெயவீர கூறினார். "ராஜபக்சேக்களும் இலங்கையும் சோகத்தில் முடிந்தது," என்று அவர் கூறினார். "அது அவர்களின் சொந்த செயலால் முடிந்தது."

en.title: President  Gotabaya and his Former  Prime  Minister Mahinda rarely confronted each other directly, yet they disagreed on everything, including high-stakes political gamesmanship and petty corruption
body_lines: The mob was bashing on the gates of the Sri Lankan prime minister’s official residence, its size and fury swelling dangerously.

For weeks, Mahinda Rajapaksa, the 76-year-old prime minister, had been under pressure to resign as the economy imploded and protests erupted. The brother of the president, Gotabaya, and a patriarch of his own political dynasty, Mahinda was once hailed as appachchi, the beloved father of the people. Now he was huddled in his second-floor bedroom, accompanied by relatives who frantically called army officers, pleading to be rescued.

Outside the gates, anti-government protesters who had been attacked earlier by Mahinda’s supporters were taking their revenge — rioting, burning buses and torching hundreds of homes owned by allies of the Rajapaksas. A lawmaker from their party was beaten to death, his body dragged through the streets.
That day, May 9, was one of the most violent and chaotic in recent Sri Lankan history. But it was precipitated by years of turmoil inside the house of Rajapaksa.

The Rajapaksa brothers have dominated politics here for most of the last 20 years. After helping Mahinda win the presidency in 2005, his brothers Chamal, Gotabaya and Basil took over ministries that controlled three-quarters of the national budget and built popular support despite allegations of human rights abuses and corruption. But by 2019, when Gotabaya became president, the family was marred by infighting and dysfunction that would drive South Asia’s most developed nation into ruin.

In interviews, current and former ministers, foreign diplomats and Rajapaksa confidants, some of whom spoke for the first time as they saw the family splinter, said Gotabaya and Mahinda, and their respective factions, clashed over ministerial appointments and agricultural policies, investment deals and political favors. As the economy went into free fall this year, Mahinda, backed by several Rajapaksa scions, resisted Gotabaya’s wish that he step aside.

Distrust deepened to the point that members of Mahinda’s inner circle, besieged in his compound May 9, felt that the president had abandoned them. Udayanga Weeratunga, a cousin who was with the prime minister, and another family aide who was present, told The Washington Post that they suspect Gotabaya’s supporters in the army purposefully delayed coming to their aid for six hours.

Gotabaya is clinging to power after replacing his brother with a new prime minister, who revealed this week that Sri Lanka has less than $1 million in foreign reserves, dwindling medical supplies and almost no fuel.
Sri Lanka faces “total destruction,” former president Maithripala Sirisena said. “The country has learned a lesson about dynastic politics.”

When Mahinda, the son of a wealthy rice and coconut farmer who was active in politics, ran for parliament in 1970, he was following in the tradition of the few elite families that dominate Sri Lanka, a lush teardrop-shape island off the coast of India.

“You cannot win [in politics] if you’re not from an established family,” said Razeen Sally, a professor at the National University of Singapore. “So the system is left to established insiders who can pillage the state.”

The second of nine children, Mahinda was charismatic, loved crowds and stuck close to his younger brother, Basil, who is considered the family’s political strategist. Their middle brother, Gotabaya, was always different: aloof, politically inexperienced, a teetotaler and vegetarian who spent 21 years in the military. “He would visit the ancestral home only during New Year,” recalled Weeratunga, their cousin who is close to Mahinda.

The Rajapaksas ran the country like a family business during Mahinda’s 10-year presidency, starting in 2005. He named Gotabaya defense secretary while Basil and their oldest brother, Chamal, were placed in charge of irrigation and economic development. Sri Lanka enjoyed years of growth, fueled by a mountain of foreign debt.
Mahinda enjoyed the adulation of voters, who approved of his bloody but decisive victory in a 26-year civil war against Tamil rebels and his frequent appeals to Sinhalese Buddhist nationalism.

But allegations of corruption, including questionable deals with Chinese state companies and officials, swirled around Mahinda. Gotabaya was also implicated, though to a lesser extent, and faced scrutiny over the 2006 purchase of MiG fighters from Ukraine.

Sankhitha Gunaratne, deputy executive director of Transparency International Sri Lanka, said Mahinda and Basil have faced numerous accusations, including diverting tsunami relief aid and using public funds to buy land, but many cases have stalled or been withdrawn. “The alleged Rajapaksa corruption is like a large tree that provides shade to many people,” she said.
In 2021, a leaked trove of financial documents known as the Pandora Papers revealed that a niece of the Rajapaksa brothers had millions of dollars hidden in offshore accounts.

Amid growing anger over the Rajapaksas’ alleged cronyism and corruption, Mahinda lost a bid for a third term in 2015. Almost immediately, an eclectic coalition of pro-Western business executives, military hard-liners and Buddhist monks identified a new candidate: Gotabaya Rajapaksa.

It quickly became apparent that Gotabaya, backed by new political sponsors, would clash with Mahinda. The men rarely confronted each other directly, yet they disagreed on everything, including high-stakes political gamesmanship and petty corruption, family confidants said.

Dilith Jayaweera, a media magnate who is widely credited with launching Gotabaya’s candidacy, remembers an incident from 2018 when he was called by Mahinda to Gotabaya’s home. Mahinda had put Gotabaya’s name on the title to an illegally built resort so that a powerful monk, a political ally, could get free electricity. The scandal was about to leak and, as was often the case, Mahinda was reluctant to tell his brother, so he nudged Jayaweera to break the news to him.
Gotabaya was “livid,” Jayaweera said, and stormed off to a Buddhist temple, refusing to share a car with his brother.

In October 2018, a constitutional crisis erupted when Sirisena, then president, fired his prime minister, Ranil Wickremesinghe, and replaced him with Mahinda, who he had defeated at the polls just three years earlier. The capital was tense as both men made claims on the country’s No. 2 job and rumors swirled that Wickremesinghe might be removed by force.
Fearing Mahinda and Basil were trying to outflank him and engineer their own return to power, Gotabaya secretly met Wickremesinghe to pledge his support.
Soon after, the Supreme Court ruled against Mahinda’s claim, and he backed down. The family had no option but to support Gotabaya.

In the run-up to elections, terrorist attacks by Muslim extremists rocked Sri Lanka, galvanizing Sinhalese Buddhist support around the former military man. On the campaign trail, Gotabaya spoke of security, good governance and development, pitching himself as a technocrat, and Colombo, with its emerging skyline of Indian- and Chinese-funded skyscrapers, as the next Singapore. He won in a landslide.

On the day of his swearing-in on Nov. 19, 2019, Gotabaya signaled a break from his family. He refused to wear a red “sataka,” the Rajapaksa clan’s signature scarf, favoring a short-sleeved shirt. Unlike Mahinda, who printed his own image on 1,000-rupee notes while he was president, Gotabaya prohibited government offices from hanging his official portrait.
But the next day was “the beginning of the downfall,” said Nalaka Godahewa, a former financial executive who was later Gotabaya’s minister of mass media.

Gotabaya’s pro-Western business-sector backers had recommended a list of appointments, but when the president unveiled his first Cabinet, it was led by Mahinda as prime minister and stocked with Basil and Mahinda loyalists. They enacted steep tax cuts and argued against seeking aid from the International Monetary Fund despite mounting debt. Gotabaya personally pushed through a ban on chemical fertilizers that hurt crop yields, just as global food prices soared.

Mahinda’s supporters said they had shaped the Cabinet only to be undermined by Gotabaya’s appointments. In several instances, the government issued trade policies that were retracted within 24 hours. “You had ministers fighting secretaries,” Weeratunga said. “Fighting permeated the administration.”
The Rajapaksas were united on one issue: A constitutional amendment passed in 2020 that weakened commissions investigating corruption and granted the president far-reaching powers over the courts.
By early 2022, the economy was in free fall. Foodstuffs like rice doubled in price from a year prior. Gasoline and electricity were in short supply. Foreign currency reserves were running out.

In April, nightly demonstrations took root in the capital demanding that the Rajapaksas leave politics, and some turned violent. Gotabaya’s entire Cabinet — which included Basil, the finance minister; the elder brother, Chamal; and Mahinda’s son — resigned, giving Gotabaya a chance to form a new government. Sri Lanka needed a stable image to present to foreign lenders and negotiate an urgent bailout.
But Mahinda, the prime minister, resisted calls from the opposition and even signals from the president to quit.

Gotabaya didn’t force the issue. “G.R. would say, ‘He knows what I want,’ ” said Godahewa, who joined the Cabinet after several Rajapaksas departed. “He felt he needed the support of Basil and Mahinda.”

With pressure mounting on Mahinda, his supporters organized a May 9 rally at Temple Trees, the prime minister’s compound. The patriarch, feeling deflated and mulling resigning, suddenly seemed energized, according to two family insiders and videos of the event.

“As a leader who has always listened to the people, I now ask you: What needs to be done?” Mahinda said to thousands of supporters sitting cross-legged in a chandelier-lit hall. “You must stay!” the crowd roared. “Does that mean I shouldn’t resign?” he asked again, soaking in calls to fight on.
When the rally ended, supporters streamed out of Temple Trees with steel rods and wooden sticks, beating anti-government protesters and sparking a bloody backlash that shocked the nation.

Holed up in Temple Trees with his sons, who had urged him to stay, Mahinda told his speechwriter at 4 p.m. that he was resigning. The speechwriter spread the news to the media, but that didn’t stop the violence, said two people inside the compound.
Despite the family’s pleas, the army didn’t send reinforcements until 11 p.m., after protesters had already breached a gate. At 4 a.m., Mahinda was evacuated by soldiers to a military base.

“Mahinda understood this stalling was deliberate,” said Weeratunga, who accused Gotabaya of trying to intimidate his brothers. But two ministers who were by the president’s side that day said he furiously called military officers to no avail.
“He could neither control the army nor police,” Godahewa said.

Godahewa and foreign diplomats said army commander Shavendra Silva — who has been in frequent touch with Western officials — was reluctant to deploy his forces for fear of being seen as ordering a military crackdown.

The absence of the military that day widened the fissures among the brothers. In a speech to parliament this week, Chamal chastised Mahinda for not leaving politics in 2015. And at a recent meeting of the Rajapaksas’ party members, family allies angrily asked why they were not protected on May 9 in a rare display of discord. “How Gotabaya treats the party now will decide the direction of the people’s wrath,” Weeratunga said.
On May 12, an embattled and isolated Gotabaya named a new prime minister: Ranil Wickremesinghe, the man he secretly met with in 2018 when he first jockeyed for the position against his brother.

Four years later, Sri Lanka’s most powerful family was crumbling — maybe for good, said Jayaweera, the media magnate.“The Rajapaksas, and Sri Lanka, ended in tragedy,” he said. “It ended because of their own doing.”
